response.justification: James Smith is a skilled polyglot software engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js experience and a solid track record in distributed systems and software craftsmanship, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ programming experience; Smith's resume shows only roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list. Additionally, while he has worked with high-throughput microservices and some infrastructure, he lacks the 5 years of large-scale infrastructure or cloud compute platform experience, and has no demonstrated cloud technical leadership or mentoring of DevOps/cloud engineers. His background is fundamentally web and application development, not systems or platform engineering.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture: consistent application of TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and pairing across multiple roles.
Exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests per second, low-latency async I/O with DLang).
Active knowledge-sharer: organized workshops, conference talks, and open-source project maintainer, suggesting potential as a technical mentor.
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient: the role requires 8 years minimum; the candidate has approximately 6 months of C/C++ and C++ is absent from his skills list entirely.
No cloud platform or compute infrastructure leadership: AWS CDK usage at Optilyz does not constitute the 5 years of large-scale infrastructure or cloud compute architecture the role demands.

No formal technical leadership in a cloud or DevOps context: preferred qualification of 2+ years mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers is unmet; leadership experience is limited to open-source project founding.
